summary: chain-api-sdk republishes multichain-crypto-wallet under a new name and silently pulls in undocumented dependency mchain-sdk
details: chain-api-sdk@0.2.11 is a verbatim clone of the multichain-crypto-wallet package (same README, same source tree, same repository URL pointing to iamnotstatic/multichain-crypto-wallet) but is published to npm under a different name by an unrelated author (abdulfataisuleiman67@gmail.com). package.json adds an undocumented dependency `mchain-sdk` (^4.2.5), and dist/index.js unconditionally `require("mchain-sdk")` at module top level and re-exports its `mci` and `multiChainInterface` symbols. The README does not mention mchain-sdk or these symbols. Any consumer importing chain-api-sdk therefore transitively loads mchain-sdk on require, whose code executes in the installer's process. The visible source is a decoy identical to a legitimate unrelated library; the effective payload carrier is the auto-loaded external dependency, whose contents live outside this tarball and are not part of the upstream project this package impersonates.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
